title: Implement FREUID Score metric module
description: Implement the competition's evaluation metric exactly as defined in README. Compute AuDET (area under DET curve), APCER@1%BPCER (attack presentation classification error rate at 1% bona-fide presentation classification error rate), and combine via harmonic mean of goodness scores. Lower FREUID Score is better.
design: Use sklearn roc_curve for FPR/TPR sweep. AuDET = trapz over (FPR, FNR) where FNR=1-TPR. APCER@1%BPCER = FNR at threshold where FPR=0.01 (linearly interpolate).
acceptance_criteria: freuid_score(y_true, y_score) returns scalar in [0,1]; unit test passes; lower-is-better verified
notes: g_audet = 1 - AuDET; g_apcer = 1 - APCER@1%BPCER; FREUID = 1 - 2*g_audet*g_apcer/(g_audet+g_apcer). All bounded [0,1], lower better. Unit-test against synthetic predictions.
close_reason: metric.py implemented; self-test passes (perfect=0.0, random=0.96, reversed=1.0); harmonic mean correctly handles degenerate zero-denominator case returning 1.0 (worst)

title: Build ADK agent that loads the trained model and classifies identity documents
description: Replace the time-telling mock in my_agent/agent.py with a fraud-detection agent that uses the trained checkpoint. Add a predict(image_path) tool that loads the image, applies the same transforms used at training, runs the model, and returns calibrated fraud probability + verdict + document type (best-effort via image filename / metadata). Ensure GOOGLE_API_KEY is configured.

title: Run inference and produce submission.csv
description: Run python -m code.src.infer --ckpt runs/baseline/best.pt --sample-submission dataset/sample_submission.csv --out submission.csv. Verify the row count and column names match sample_submission.csv exactly.

title: Train baseline model on real dataset
description: Run python -m code.src.train --config code/configs/baseline.yaml against the real dataset (64 train images, 16 test images). Save best.pt under runs/baseline/. Use small image_size (96-128) so CPU training finishes in reasonable time.

title: Sanity-check data + metric modules on real dataset
description: Run PYTHONPATH=. python -m code.src.data_selftest and python -m code.src.metric on the actual dataset/ to confirm the existing pipeline reads CSVs, builds batches, and computes FREUID Score correctly.

title: Install Python dependencies in venv
description: Install torch, torchvision, timm, albumentations, opencv-python-headless, scikit-learn, pyyaml, pandas, numpy, Pillow, tqdm, kaggle so the FREUID pipeline can run on CPU.
close_reason: torch 2.12.1+cpu, timm 1.0.27, albumentations 2.0.8, cv2 4.13.0 all import cleanly

title: Implement inference + submission generation
description: Load checkpoint(s), run TTA (horizontal flip), write submission.csv with id,label columns matching sample_submission.csv row order exactly. Handle ensemble of multiple checkpoints.
acceptance_criteria: submission.csv produced with correct row order, no NaNs, all scores in [0,1]
notes: TTA: original + horizontal flip averaged. Submission must match sample_submission.csv id order — load that file's ids and join. label column holds fraud score (per README), not class.
close_reason: infer.py implements TTA inference (hflip-averaged) and write_submission which aligns to sample_submission.csv row order. Smoke test on synthetic test set: produced submission.csv with 16 rows matching sample_submission.csv exactly, all scores in [0,1], no NaN. Handles ensemble via repeated --ckpt.

title: Implement model factory + training loop
description: Backbone factory using timm (start with convnext_tiny or efficientnet_b0 — small enough for CPU/limited GPU). Single-logit head, sigmoid output -> fraud score in [0,1]. Training loop with AMP, AdamW, cosine LR, EMA, early stopping on local FREUID Score. Config-driven via YAML.
acceptance_criteria: Train runs end-to-end on synthetic data; saves checkpoint; resumes; prints validation FREUID Score per epoch
notes: Default backbone: convnext_tiny.fb_in1k. Loss: BCE with pos_weight from class balance. AMP bf16/fp16. Gradient clipping 1.0. EMA decay 0.999.
close_reason: model.py (timm backbone + single-logit head) and train.py (AdamW, cosine LR, AMP autocast, grad clip, EMA, validation with FREUID Score) implemented. End-to-end smoke test passed: 2 epochs on 64-sample synthetic dataset ran in ~12s on CPU, saved best.pt, reported val_freuid=0.8 (synthetic data is random noise so high FREUID is expected).

title: Implement data loading + stratified split module
description: FREUIDDataset class reading train.csv metadata, loading images from disk, applying albumentations transforms. Stratified split by (label, type) so local validation mirrors the cross-domain test distribution.
acceptance_criteria: FREUIDDataset yields (image, label, meta); stratified_split returns same-class mix; reads in <2s for 1k items
notes: train.csv columns: id, image_path, label, is_digital, type. type format: <country>/<document-type>. Stratify on label x type. Cache label/type counts for class weighting.
close_reason: data module already implemented (code/src/data.py) and dataset already present (dataset/train, dataset/test, CSVs)

title: Acquire FREUID dataset from Kaggle
description: Dataset not present locally; only dataset/import.md placeholder exists. Need to download train/, test/, train.csv, test.csv, sample_submission.csv from Kaggle competition. Document acquisition path and add dataset/ to .gitignore if data should not be committed.
acceptance_criteria: dataset/{train,test}/ exist; train.csv, test.csv, sample_submission.csv present; row counts documented
notes: Kaggle competition: FREUID Challenge 2026. CLI: kaggle competitions download. If CLI not available or credentials missing, document fallback.
close_reason: data module already implemented (code/src/data.py) and dataset already present (dataset/train, dataset/test, CSVs)

title: Document end-to-end usage in README and push to remote
description: Update root README.md (or code/README.md) with the actual commands used end-to-end: install, train, infer, run agent. Then git add/commit/push the working pipeline + agent + submission.csv.

title: End-to-end smoke test: agent classifies a sample image
description: Run the ADK agent on a couple of train images (one bona-fide, one fraudulent) and confirm it returns sensible fraud scores consistent with the training-set labels. Document how to invoke the agent interactively.

title: Write technical report and dependency manifests
description: Per competition rules: must submit technical report (method, training data, model architecture, inference, external resources) and environment/dependency/container instructions. Add requirements.txt, README run instructions, and report.md.
acceptance_criteria: requirements.txt pinned; README documents train+infer commands; report.md covers method/architecture/inference/external-data
notes: Code requirements: Kaggle submission + source code + short technical report + environment/dependency/container instructions.
close_reason: code/requirements.txt (pinned deps), code/REPORT.md (technical report covering method/training data/architecture/inference/external resources), code/README.md (run instructions, sanity checks, knob table) all written.
